latest.title: Faron Pharmaceuticals: Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd: Issuance of warrants
latest.content:
Today 06:00
Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd | Company announcement | April 30, 2026 at 09:00:00 EEST
TURKU, FINLAND â€" Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd. (AIM: FARN, First North: FARON), a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, announces that it has issued to IPF Fund II SCA, SICAV-FIAR (â€œIPFâ€) 321,523 additional warrants over ordinary shares in the Company (â€œWarrantsâ€), following the the amortisation of the first and second tranche convertible bonds to Heights Capital Management, Inc.at EUR 0.4516 announced on 13 April 2026.
The subscription price of all of the Warrants held by IPF is reset to EUR 0.4516 (â€œ
Subscription Price
â€). As described in the Warrant Terms and Conditions announcement of 8 April 2024, in the event of any future share issue at a price lower than the Subscription Price, the Subscription Price of the Warrants will be adjusted to that lower subsequent price.
Following this issues, IPF will have an interest in 3,641,467 Warrants, representing approximately 1.83% of the issued share capital in the Company. Each Warrant entitles its holder to subscribe for one new share in the Company. The Warrants may be exercised for a period of seven years from the original date of issue.
For more information, please contact:
IR Partners, Finland
(Media)
Kare Laukkanen
+358 50 553 9535 / +44 7 469 766 223
kare.laukkanen@irpartners.fi
FINN Partners, US
(Media)
Alyssa Paldo
+1 847 791-8085
alyssa.paldo@finnpartners.com
Cairn Financial Advisers LLP
(Nominated Adviser and Broker)
Sandy Jamieson, Jo Turner
+44 (0) 207 213 0880
Sisu Partners Oy
(Certified Adviser on Nasdaq First North)
Juha KarttunenJukka J¤rvel¤
+358 (0)40 555 4727+358 (0)50 553 8990
About
bexmarilimab
Bexmarilimab is Faronâ€™s wholly owned, investigational immunotherapy designed to overcome resistance to existing treatments and optimize clinical outcomes, by targeting myeloid cell function and igniting the immune system. Bexmarilimab binds to Clever-1, an immunosuppressive receptor found on macrophages leading to tumor growth and metastases (i.e. helps cancer evade the immune system). By targeting the Clever-1 receptor on macrophages, bexmarilimab alters the tumor microenvironment, reprogramming macrophages from an immunosuppressive (M2) state to an immunostimulatory (M1) one, upregulating interferon production and priming the immune system to attack tumors and sensitizing cancer cells to standard of care.
About Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd
Faron (AIM: FARN, First North: FARON) is a global, clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, focused on tackling cancers via novel immunotherapies. Its mission is to bring the promise of immunotherapy to a broader population by uncovering novel ways to control and harness the power of the immune system. The companyâ€™s lead asset, bexmarilimab, is a novel macrophage-guiding immunotherapy being investigated in multiple oncology settings. Further information is available at
www.faron.com
.

company_30s.what_they_do: Faron Pharmaceuticals develops antibody‑based immuno‑oncology therapies, focusing on novel treatments for solid tumours.
company_30s.why_they_matter: They target the global oncology market, which is worth billions, and their lead candidate is in a late‑stage trial that could unlock a large commercial opportunity if approved.
company_30s.current_state: Still in clinical development with no revenue. The company has raised fresh capital through a €40 million rights issue to extend its cash runway and is actively pushing key milestones for its main drug. The stock is trading far below its historical highs, reflecting a heavy burn and long‑term development horizon.

rns_narrative: Faron has just closed a €40 million rights issue, confirming it has the cash to fund the next phase of its lead programme. The company also announced a new CTO to accelerate development and has updated its financial calendar to show upcoming milestones. These moves signal that the firm is trying to stabilise its capital base and keep the pipeline on track.

The announcements cluster around three themes: (1) capital strengthening and governance updates, (2) leadership changes aimed at speeding development, and (3) transparency around upcoming trial dates and regulatory meetings.
The narrative shows the company is actively managing risk and positioning itself for a potential breakthrough, which could be the catalyst the market is waiting for.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism around a new drug milestone.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of biotech‑fans point to the successful rights issue and fresh leadership as positive signs that the company is on track for a breakthrough.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Critics highlight the lack of revenue, high burn and the uncertainty of the trial’s outcome. They worry that the capital raise could be a temporary stop‑gap.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has stayed neutral, with a slight lift after the rights‑issue announcement. No dramatic spikes in chatter have appeared.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for Faron is flat – no recent search activity, giving the stock a high surprise potential if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: The first set of Phase‑II data is due in the next 2–3 months; a positive read could spark a bounce.
The upcoming regulatory meeting (AGM) will set the timeline for the next milestone and could signal confidence.
Share registration and the completion of the rights‑issue offer could tighten supply dynamics and reduce volatility.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike of 30–50 % above the 50‑day average, a break above the 30‑day moving average, or a clear price bounce to the 50% of peak level.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is likely still in the early capitulation phase. The APEX score (not shown) would help confirm a tight compression – a good sign that mean reversion is imminent. Confirmation comes when price moves above the last support level and volume confirms it.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If we treat this as a deep crash (green‑level), historical returns average 39 % with a 40 % win rate. The current price is roughly 30 % of its all‑time high, so a rebound to 50 % of the peak would give a 67 % upside from now. Reaching the peak would deliver a 233 % gain. The upside is large compared to the 30 % downside risk, giving an asymmetry of about 8:1.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risks are trial failure, regulatory rejection, or a cash crunch if the rights‑issue does not fully cover burn. If the company fails to hit its next milestone, the price could slide further to 20 % of the peak, a 80 % loss from the current level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 30 %. Potential gain: 233 % (to peak) or 67 % (to 50 % of peak). Risk‑reward ratio roughly 1 : 7.7, strongly favourable for a contrarian long.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed volume spike and a price break above the 30‑day moving average, ideally at a support level that has held for at least 2–3 weeks. Allocate 5–10 % of discretionary capital to this position. Set a stop 25 % below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the Phase‑II data release or the AGM decision. If the stock fails to move above the 50 % of peak level within 90 days, reassess the thesis.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks identified at this time; the only real downside is a failed trial or a regulatory setback that could wipe out the remaining upside.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Faron sits at about 30 % of its peak, the rights issue shows it has capital to reach the next milestone, and a positive trial outcome could deliver a 200 % upside – a highly asymmetric contrarian play.
